conjecture_title: Informal Problem (projective plane structure in near-extremal constructions)
paper_title: A note on intersecting hypergraphs with large cover number
summary: The informal problem asks whether near-extremal intersecting r-partite hypergraphs H with \tau(H) close to r must contain large pieces from a finite projective plane, or whether genuinely different constructions exist. No follow-up paper specifically resolving either direction was found. The broader literature on Ryser's conjecture confirms that truncated projective planes remain essentially the only known near-extremal constructions, consistent with the authors' observation, but no structural theorem characterising near-extremal examples has been established.
search_queries: intersecting r-partite hypergraph cover number projective plane near-extremal Haxell Scott 1609.05458
intersecting hypergraph cover number tau projective plane construction extremal arxiv
Ryser conjecture r-partite intersecting hypergraph cover number projective plane 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up found specifically addressing this informal problem. Related papers checked: arXiv:2503.14918 (Bucic-Jain-Sivashankar 2025) addresses the Erdos-Lovasz problem with a vertex constraint and does not discuss r-partite near-extremal structure; arXiv:2011.04444 (Barat 2020) studies maximal covering number in the non-partite setting; arXiv:2001.04132 (Bishnoi-Das-Morris-Szabo 2020) addresses Ryser's conjecture for t-intersecting hypergraphs. Projective plane constructions (truncated projective planes) remain the only known near-extremal families.
